title: The Social Contract
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
excerpt: Rousseau rejects natural right to authority and argues for a pact among citizens as the legitimate source of sovereign power, introducing the concept of the "general will." The treatise profoundly influenced the French Revolution.
whoShouldRead: Readers interested in Philosophy and Political Science and History
keyIdeas: Central thesis of The Social Contract
keyTakeaways: Understand the core argument and its implications
